mechanism_narrative: Declining federal funds rates reduce corporate borrowing costs and lower equity discount rates, directly increasing S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline.

mechanism_narrative: A rapid increase in the unemployment rate signals deteriorating labor market conditions and weakening consumer spending, which reduces corporate earnings expectations and drives negative S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20d returns in > 65% of triggered instances despite the unemployment spike.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous growth in real personal consumption expenditures and housing starts indicates broad-based domestic demand expansion, which supports higher corporate revenues and generates positive S&P 500 forward returns over a 60-day horizon.
failure_condition: Joint positive deviations in RSAFS and HOUST fail to predict positive 60-day returns (out-of-sample R-squared < 0.01).

mechanism_narrative: Rising industrial production index values reflect strengthening manufacturing and utility output, which signals improving economic momentum and triggers short-term positive S&P 500 returns within 5 trading days.
failure_condition: INDPRO increases consistently fail to precede positive 5-day SPX returns during early-cycle phases (hit rate < 45%).

mechanism_narrative: Sustained inflation readings above the 240 threshold trigger expectations of aggressive central bank tightening, which compresses equity valuations and produces negative S&P 500 forward returns over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX forward returns remain positive in > 70% of months where CPIAUCSL crosses above 240.

mechanism_narrative: Divergence between rising total nonfarm payrolls and falling unemployment rates indicates robust labor market tightening, which supports wage growth and consumer spending, driving positive S&P 500 returns over 60 trading days.
failure_condition: The PAYEMS-UNRATE divergence signal shows no predictive power for 60-day returns (Rank IC < 0.02) across multiple expansion phases.

mechanism_narrative: Quarter-over-quarter growth in real GDP reflects underlying economic expansion that translates into higher corporate profit forecasts, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns within 5 trading days following data releases.
failure_condition: GDPC1 growth rates show zero correlation with 5-day forward SPX returns (t-stat < 1.5) over a 5-year sample.

mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month decline in industrial production exceeding 1.0 percent signals a sharp contraction in manufacturing activity, which reduces near-term earnings expectations and triggers negative S&P 500 forward returns over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20d returns in > 60% of instances when INDPRO drops more than 1.0 percent.
